evidence.identifying_name: Target program: CASTLE-125-7.c - Array declarations and initialization
Target program: CASTLE-125-7.c - Array read accesses (all in-bounds)
Infer source: bufferOverrunUtils.ml - Array declaration and size tracking for C arrays
evidence.snippet:
and decl_local_array ({pname; caller_pname; node_hash; location} as model_env) loc typ ~length
    ?stride ~inst_num ~represents_multiple_values ~dimension mem =
  let size = Option.value_map ~default:Itv.top ~f:Itv.of_int_lit length in
  ...
  let offset = Itv.zero in
  Dom.Val.of_c_array_alloc allocsite ~stride ~offset ~size ~traces

evidence.identifying_name: Infer source: bufferOverrunProofObligations.ml - Core bounds checking logic
evidence.snippet:
let check : t -> checked_condition =
 fun c ->
    let real_idx = ItvPure.plus c.offset c.idx in
    let size =
      let size_nonneg = ItvPure.make_non_negative c.size in
      if c.last_included then ItvPure.succ size_nonneg else size_nonneg
    in
    let not_overrun = ItvPure.lt_sem real_idx size in
    let not_underrun = ItvPure.le_sem ItvPure.zero real_idx in
    if Boolean.is_true not_overrun && Boolean.is_true not_underrun then
      {report_issue_type= NotIssue; propagate= false}

evidence.identifying_name: CASTLE benchmark CSV: CWE-125 detection results for all files

reasoning: CWE-125 (Out-of-bounds Read) is detected by Infer's --bufferoverrun checker. The checker tracks the size and offset of each array allocation using the ArrayBlk domain (arrayBlk.ml:18). For C stack arrays like 'int a[100]' and 'double b[200]', the size is computed at declaration time from the type length (bufferOverrunUtils.ml:75). On each Load instruction, check_expr_for_array_access is invoked (bufferOverrunChecker.ml:306), which processes Exp.Lindex expressions by calling BufferOverrunUtils.Check.lindex (bufferOverrunUtils.ml:297) to evaluate the index and array expressions, then checks whether 0 <= offset + idx < size (bufferOverrunProofObligations.ml:329-342). In CASTLE-125-7.c, all loops access arrays strictly within their declared bounds: a has 100 elements with accesses at indices [0,99] and [0,49]; b has 200 elements with accesses at indices [0,199] and [0,99]. The bufferoverrun checker correctly determines that not_overrun (idx < size) and not_underrun (0 <= idx) are both true for every access, resulting in a NotIssue verdict. The empty report.json for this file is correct behavior—there is no buffer over-read vulnerability present. The benchmark CSV entry (CWE Match = False, Marked By Infer = False) accurately reflects that Infer's analysis found no CWE-125 violations, which is a true negative, not a soundness failure.

minimal_test_case.description: This minimal test case demonstrates the same pattern as CASTLE-125-7.c: two arrays of different types and sizes where all loop accesses are within bounds. Infer's bufferoverrun checker should correctly report no issues.
